place: Blackwood CDP, New Jersey, New Jersey

facts.text: Blackwood was originally called Blackwoodtown, settled around 1750 by John Blackwood, a Scottish immigrant who ran mills.
The arrival of the Gloucester Branch of the Pennsylvania and Reading Railroad in 1891 turned Blackwood into a commuter suburb of Camden and Philadelphia.
Blackwood Lake served as a summer resort from 1891 until 1932, offering a retreat for nearby city dwellers.
The Blackwood Historic District, covering 42 acres along the Black Horse Pike, was added to the National Register of Historic Places in 1989 for its community significance.
Camden County College’s radio station WDBK, broadcasting on 91.5 FM since 1977, calls Blackwood home.
Blackwood first appeared as an unincorporated community in the 1950 U.S. Census.
